step_by_step_reasoning: Data scientists upload models via a UI Portal connected to a Platform API (EC2). The API stores metadata in OpenSearch, files in S3, and publishes build tasks to MSK (Kafka). A build runner (EC2) pulls these resources, gets dependencies from Artifactory, builds Docker images, stores them in ECR, and deploys to SageMaker. Status updates trigger EventBridge, Lambda, MSK, and a Fargate consumer, which updates OpenSearch and sends the final status back to the user.

graph.name: Experian Automated Model Ingestion and Deployment
graph.notes: Automates onboarding of custom Python models from months to days using containerized pipelines and SageMaker endpoints.

nodes.name: Data Scientists
nodes.notes: DATA_PEEK: Uploads Python serialized (.pkl) files and attributes.py via UI Portal.

nodes.name: Platform API
nodes.notes: Handles incoming model upload and registration requests.

nodes.name: Model Registry
nodes.notes: Metadata store tracking model status, versions, and endpoint URLs.

nodes.name: Artifact Store
nodes.notes: Stores uploaded raw model artifacts and attributes.

nodes.name: Kafka Event Bus
nodes.notes: Asynchronous event broker managing pipeline stages.

nodes.name: Build Machine
nodes.notes: WORKLOAD_PEEK: Runs Docker to containerize SageMaker-compatible images.

nodes.name: Artifactory
nodes.notes: Hosts required code and model library dependencies.

nodes.name: Model Endpoint
nodes.notes: Hosts model for scalable auto-scaling inference.

nodes.name: Status Handler
nodes.notes: Triggers on EventBridge signals to publish status changes back to MSK.

nodes.name: Status Consumer
nodes.notes: Consumes status updates, writes back to the UI portal and registry.

edges.notes: Publishes change status back to topic
Pushes build completion status to UI
